name: git-workflow
description: Enhanced git operations using lazygit, gh (GitHub CLI), and delta. Triggers on: stage changes, create PR, review PR, check issues, git diff, commit interactively, GitHub operations, rebase, stash, bisect.
capabilities: Open lazygit interactive TUI for visual git operations
Create and manage pull requests using gh CLI
View beautiful syntax-highlighted diffs with delta
Stage, commit, push, and pull changes efficiently
Manage branches, stashes, and rebase operations
List and review issues and pull requests
author: 0xDarkMatter
securitySummary: This is a legitimate git workflow documentation skill. All 109 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES triggered by markdown documentation containing git command examples. No actual security risks present. The skill only provides reference documentation for git operations.
valueStatement: Managing git operations from the terminal can be complex. This skill provides quick access to lazygit, gh CLI, and delta for interactive staging, PR creation, and beautiful diffs.
userTitle: Streamline Git Workflows with CLI Tools
limitations: Requires git, gh CLI, lazygit, and delta to be installed
Needs network access for GitHub operations like PR creation
Cannot execute git operations without user confirmation
Does not provide custom git scripting or automation

useCases.targetUser: Developers who prefer visual interfaces over command-line flags
useCases.title: Visual Git Staging and Committing
useCases.description: Use lazygit TUI to stage files, write commits, and push changes without remembering command flags.

useCases.targetUser: Developers who want to stay in terminal for GitHub workflows
useCases.title: Create Pull Requests from Terminal
useCases.description: Create, list, view, and merge pull requests using gh CLI commands directly from the terminal.

useCases.targetUser: Code reviewers and developers examining changes
useCases.title: Review Code with Beautiful Diffs
useCases.description: View git diffs with syntax highlighting, side-by-side comparison, and clean formatting using delta.

promptTemplates.title: Open Git TUI
promptTemplates.prompt: Open lazygit to stage and commit my changes
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick access to visual git interface

promptTemplates.title: Create Pull Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a pull request with title 'Feature: Add user authentication' and description 'This PR adds user login and registration functionality.'
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a pull request from terminal

promptTemplates.title: View Beautiful Diff
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the diff of my changes with delta syntax highlighting

promptTemplates.title: List Open Pull Requests
promptTemplates.prompt: List all open pull requests for this repository

bestPractices: Use lazygit for complex staging operations to avoid accidental commits
Configure delta as your default pager for consistent diff viewing
Use `gh pr create --web` to verify PR appearance before submission
antiPatterns: Do not use `git push --force` on shared branches
Avoid large commits; break changes into logical pieces
Do not skip writing meaningful commit messages

faq.question: What tools does this skill require?
faq.answer: This skill requires git, gh CLI, lazygit, and delta to be installed on your system.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without network access?
faq.answer: Local git operations like staging, committing, and viewing diffs work offline. GitHub operations like creating PRs require network access.

faq.question: How do I exit lazygit?
faq.answer: Press `q` to quit lazygit. Use `?` to see all available keybindings.

faq.question: Can delta show side-by-side diffs?
faq.answer: Yes, use `git diff | delta --side-by-side` for parallel diff viewing.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my git configuration?
faq.answer: No, this skill only provides commands and documentation. Configuration changes require explicit user action.

faq.question: Can I create issues with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, use `gh issue create --title "Your title"` to create issues from the terminal.
